title: iberis linifolia subsp. violletii
intro: Iberis linifolia subsp. violletii, commonly known as Dwarf Candytuft, is a plant species in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its compact growth, delicate white flowers, and linear leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Iberis linifolia subsp. violletii include its potential to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and promote overall respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
